CheckTitle: Ensure software OATH tokens are disabled
Description: Checks whether software OATH token authentication method is enabled. Software OATH tokens (third-party TOTP apps) are less secure than Microsoft Authenticator with number matching and should be disabled in favor of phishing-resistant methods.
Risk: Software OATH tokens generate time-based one-time passwords that can be phished through adversary-in-the-middle attacks. Unlike Microsoft Authenticator or FIDO2, TOTP codes can be relayed in real-time by attackers.

Remediation.Recommendation: Disable the software OATH token authentication method and migrate users to Microsoft Authenticator (with number matching) or FIDO2 security keys.

SourceNotes: AI-generated framework mappings — verify before citing in compliance reports.
